human.note: The comparison is broad and useful, but it materially characterizes MINT as stateless with only short-lived state and makes other state/feedback assertions that are not established by the cited passages.

grounding_issues: MINT 'three nested settings' (1) tool use only (2) lazy user (3) informative NL feedback — not stated in supplied evidence passages.</item>
"turn limit k ∈ {1,2,3,4,5}" — specific values not present in supplied evidence; only "a limit on the number of interaction turns k" is mentioned.</item>
"lazy (≤1 binary 'this is not what I want' reply)" — the quoted phrase and the binary/≤1 characterization are not in supplied evidence.</item>
"ALFWorld (a TextWorld-based embodied household simulator)" — ALFWorld mention and TextWorld characterization not in supplied evidence.</item>
"HumanEval/MBPP test suites" — these benchmarks not named in supplied evidence.</item>
"Code is one flexible, generic tool across very different tasks (reasoning, code gen, decision-making)" — task categories and 'generic tool' characterization added beyond supplied evidence.</item>
"Typed signatures, e.g., return_delivered_order_items(...)" — specific example function name not in supplied evidence.</item>
"an ablation compares simulated vs. lazy feedback to attribute gains" — not described in supplied evidence.</item>
"deterministic Python transitions Tdb" partially cited but "stochastic LM sampling Tuser" assigned to user's earlier synthesis without direct support in [18].</item>
MINT "state is short-lived within a trajectory" characterization — supported only implicitly; sourced from ev_4b48a5da3b359ac1 which says nothing about persistence.</item>
judge.rationale: The answer correctly addresses all five requested comparison axes and is factually reasonable. Grounding fails because multiple specific details (turn limit values, the "three nested settings" labels, HumanEval/MBPP/ALFWorld names, specific quoted phrases like "this is not what I want") are added beyond what the supplied evidence passages actually state.
